candidate_llm_application: PAJ-NTAUB-PANEL: panel-bordered narrative cascade for long-form generation. K panel segments P_1..P_K each preceded by frame-border F_i^open=[PANEL_i_START,pos=i/K,topic_anchor=Θ_i] + followed by F_i^close=[PANEL_i_END,anchor_echo=Θ_i_verified]. Cross-panel attention restricted to consecutive frame-echo signal.
rule: hit = (kw>=2 OR sem>=0.7 OR func>=0.7); total_hits >= 1 -> FAIL

results.title: Chain of Agents LLMs Long-Context (NeurIPS 2024)
matched_keywords: long-form bordered-panel chain generation (partial: chain + long-context)
results.rationale: Chain-of-Agents performs segment-wise sequential agent communication with summary handoff for long-context — functionally equivalent to the panel-cascade handoff mechanism even though it lacks explicit frame-border tokens or anchor-echo verification.

results.title: NEXUSSUM Hierarchical LLM Agents Long-Form Narrative Summarization (ACL 2025)
matched_keywords: long-form bordered-panel chain generation (partial: long-form narrative)
results.rationale: NEXUSSUM is hierarchical multi-stage compression of narrative without explicit panel-border tokens or topic-anchor echo handoff; overlap is generic long-form narrative segmentation.

results.title: Game Knowledge Management Schema-Governed LLM Narrative Generation MDPI 2026
matched_keywords: frame-token topic-anchor opening closing (partial: structural anchors)
results.rationale: G-KMS uses schema-governed structural anchors with normalization-based repair — conceptually adjacent to topic-anchor verification but applied at schema/admission level, not as per-panel bracketing tokens with cross-panel attention restriction.

results.title: Hierarchical Expansion Long-Form Coherent Content OpenCredo
matched_keywords: frame-token topic-anchor opening closing (partial: topic anchors per section)
results.rationale: Outline-then-expand with per-section topic anchors overlaps in spirit with panel topic-anchors but lacks explicit frame-border open/close tokens, anchor-echo verification, and restricted cross-panel attention mechanism.

results.title: Narrative Weaver Long-Range Visual Consistency Multi-Modal (2603.06688)
matched_keywords: panel-bordered serialized narrative cascade (panel-based serialized narrative)
results.rationale: Narrative Weaver explicitly uses panel-based serialized narrative with cross-panel conditioning for long-range consistency — strong keyword, semantic, and functional overlap with the panel-bordered cascade mechanism even though the modality is visual.

results.title: Survey LLMs Story Generation (Findings EMNLP 2025)
matched_keywords: panel-bordered serialized narrative cascade (panel-structured story generation)
results.rationale: EMNLP 2025 survey explicitly covers multi-stage panel-structured story generation with frame-narrative bracketing — directly subsumes the panel-bordered narrative cascade with frame tokens.

results.title: Narrative Theory-Driven LLM Story Generation Survey (2602.15851)
matched_keywords: panel-bordered serialized narrative cascade (partial: segment generation with narrative structure framing)
results.rationale: Multi-stage segment generation with explicit narrative structure framing semantically overlaps panel-bordered cascade; lacks explicit anchor-echo verification handoff but covers the framing primitive.

results.title: CascadeInfer Length-Aware Inter-Instance Cascade LLM (2025/12)
matched_keywords: handoff verification frame echo (length summary handoff)
long-form bordered-panel chain generation (cascade pipeline with stage boundary)
results.rationale: CascadeInfer is an explicit cascade pipeline with stage boundary adjustment and lightweight summary handoff between stages — directly mirrors the frame-bordered cascade with compressed handoff mechanism.

reason: Five of eight results trigger the hit rule. Narrative Weaver (#5) and EMNLP 2025 Story Generation Survey (#6) both cover panel-structured serialized narrative with frame bracketing (kw>=2, sem>=0.7, func>=0.7). CascadeInfer (#8) provides explicit cascade-with-stage-boundary + summary handoff (kw>=2, sem>=0.7, func>=0.7). Chain-of-Agents (#1) covers functional segment handoff (func>=0.7). Narrative Theory survey (#7) provides semantic coverage of multi-stage segment+structure framing (sem>=0.7). The panel-bordered narrative cascade core mechanism is already covered by prior work across three distinct clusters (panel-structured narrative, cascade-with-stage-boundary handoff, sequential agent segment handoff).
recommendation: REJECT — recombination of panel-structured narrative generation, frame bracketing, and cascade-handoff is already established. To gain novelty, candidate must move beyond bracketing+echo to a mechanistically distinct primitive (e.g., bidirectional anchor reconciliation, verifier-gated panel re-emission, or quantitative cross-panel attention budget tied to border-token entropy) not currently covered by Narrative Weaver/EMNLP survey/CascadeInfer.
